data.title: Mercedes reports narrower-than-forecast profit slump in first quarter
data.description:
Mercedes-Benz on Wednesday reported a slump in first-quarter operating profit but beat market expectations, as the German premium carmaker battles to shore up dwindling margins and revive weak demand in key markets like China.

The automaker reported first-quarter earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) of 1.9 billion euros ($2.22 billion), down 17% year on year but higher than the average analyst estimate of 1.6 billion euros, according to a poll conducted by Visible Alpha.

Steep tariffs, China woes and a rocky transition to electric vehicles have weighed heavily on German carmakers like Mercedes, whose CEO Ola Kaellenius has turned to sweeping redundancies and cost cuts to stem losses while rolling out a cascade of new models.

The Stuttgart-based company posted first-quarter revenue of 31.6 billion euros, missing analyst estimates for 31.8 billion euros.

The adjusted return on sales for Mercedes' core cars division was 4.1%, within a full-year target of between 3 and 5%, but down from 7.3% in the same quarter last year.

($1 = 0.8543 euros)

data.title: Mercedes-Benz Confirms 2026 Outlook
data.description:
Mercedes-Benz Group AG :

  • MERCEDES-BENZ - Q1 REVENUE AT EUR 31.6 BILLION (VISIBLE ALPHA ESTIMATES: EUR 31.8 MILLION)

  • MERCEDES-BENZ - CONFIRMS 2026 OUTLOOK WITH GROUP REVENUE AT PRIOR-YEAR LEVEL, GROUP EBIT SIGNIFICANTLY ABOVE PRIOR-YEAR LEVEL

  • MERCEDES-BENZ - Q1 EBIT AT EUR 1.9 BILLION (VISIBLE ALPHA ESTIMATES: EUR 1.6 BILLION)

  • MERCEDES-BENZ - OUTLOOK 2026: FREE CASH FLOW OF INDUSTRIAL BUSINESS EXPECTED SLIGHTLY BELOW 2025 LEVEL

  • MERCEDES-BENZ - Q1 FREE CASH FLOW OF INDUSTRIAL BUSINESS AT EUR 1.86 BILLION

  • MERCEDES-BENZ - OUTLOOK 2026: MERCEDES-BENZ CARS TARGETS MEDIUM-TERM SALES OF AROUND 2 MILLION VEHICLES

data.title: GJF: Strong insurance revenue growth and improved profitability, with robust capital position
data.description:
Profit before tax increased 20% year-over-year, driven by strong insurance revenue growth and improved combined ratio, while the investment result declined. The capital position remains robust, and operational targets focus on profitability and efficiency.

Original document: Gjensidige Forsikring ASA [GJF] Interim report — Apr. 29 2026

data.title: Volvo Cars' operating profit falls in Q1
data.description: Sweden-based Volvo Cars reported a fall in first-quarter operating profit on Wednesday and said it expects second-quarter profitability to be affected by continued headwinds and the production start and ramp-up of its new model EX60 .

data.title: Swedbank Q1 net profit in line with estimates
data.description: Swedish banking group Swedbank on Wednesday reported first-quarter net profit in line with estimates and said the economic outlook was uncertain due to the conflict in the Middle East.

data.title: ALMA: Revenue up 4.9% and profitability strengthened across all segments; digital share at 85.9%
data.description:
Revenue grew 4.9% to €83.1M, with digital business at 85.9% of sales and all segments improving profitability. Adjusted operating profit rose 18.6% to €20.4M, and net debt fell 25.9% to €98.6M. Otava Oy acquired a controlling stake, and the outlook anticipates stable revenue with profit growth.

Original document: Alma Media Oyj [ALMA] Interim report — Apr. 29 2026

data.title: FSECURE: Tier 1 Verizon partnership and raised outlook highlight a quarter of strategic progress amid margin pressure
data.description:
Currency neutral revenue grew 2.1% year-over-year, but reported revenue declined 2.1% to €36.3M. Adjusted EBITA margin fell to 31.8%, with profitability impacted by a shift toward lower-margin Embedded Security and restructuring costs. Major Tier 1 partnership with Verizon is expected to drive future growth.
